summary: Independent R3 systems review found the protocol and sim-core changes broadly within the allowed package boundaries, with passing typecheck, sim-core, protocol, determinism, root test, task validation, and whitespace checks. However, this cannot be accepted because bulk appointment validation does not validate collective primary-office conflicts before applying the batch: two individually valid primary appointments to the same character are rejected only as a generic post-commit invariant-violation instead of stable per-item bulk rejection reasons. A second authority/error-model gap lets sim.enfeoff-district with actor id polity:999 and grantedByPolityId 999 pass the string authority check, then fail only as an invariant-violation for a missing PolityId. Both issues violate the R3 requirement that authority and bulk semantics be explicit command validation rather than hidden invariant fallback.

tests_run.result: Observed modified protocol/sim-core/test files plus pre-existing project coordination changes; new writer handoff, route file, and tests/sim-core-m3-appointment-enfeoffment.test.ts are untracked. No product/code files were modified by this review.
No forbidden random, real-time, DOM, browser, Electron, React/Pixi, or Node IO matches in sim-core/protocol source.

tests_run.command: node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module -e M3 bulk collective primary-conflict repro
tests_run.result: Submitted sim.appoint-offices-bulk with two valid primary offices assigning the same character. Result was rejected with code invariant-violation at state.m3.offices and message 'M3 primary office holder appears in more than one primary office.' Runtime hash remained unchanged. Expected behavior is explicit bulk-command-rejected with stable per-item reason strings before commit/invariant fallback.

tests_run.command: node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module -e M3 enfeoff unknown grantor authority repro
tests_run.result: Submitted sim.enfeoff-district with actor id polity:999 and grantedByPolityId 999 in a world with only PolityId 1. Result was rejected with code invariant-violation at state.m3.administrativeDistricts[0].polityId for missing PolityId 999. Runtime hash remained unchanged. Expected behavior is explicit bad-id or authority-denied from command validation/state authority checks.
11 test files passed; 63 tests passed. Existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning for deterministic-rng-worker.ts remains non-blocking.
Node/worker hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic canaries matched; root Vitest 21 files/121 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

decisions: Review outcome is REQUEST_CHANGES and route_to is simulation_engineer because both failures are localized to sim-core command validation semantics and test coverage.
The added command/query schemas are explicit and player/AI actors share the same GameCommand kinds; the issue is not command-family asymmetry.
sim-core/protocol do not import React, Pixi, Electron, DOM, Node filesystem, or real-time APIs in the inspected paths.
state.m3 participates in canonicalization and hashing, and requestSaveV1 still intentionally refuses M3 state before emitting save bytes.
The decision scaffold query remains minimal: it exposes reason codes and executionPerformanceBps, not full hidden weights or an autonomous planner.
risks: Bulk appointment tests currently cover missing/dead item rejection and order determinism, but not collective validity after applying all items, such as duplicate primary holder assignments across different offices in one batch.

actorHasPolityAuthority accepts actor.id equality for polity:<id> without first proving that the polity exists in M3 state. Commands that use a polity id supplied directly in payload can therefore reach invariant fallback rather than returning explicit authority or bad-id errors.
Leaving these as invariant fallback paths weakens the public error model and makes client/AI callers depend on less stable internal validation messages.
requested_action: Fix sim-core command validation so bulk appointment batches validate collective primary-office exclusivity and return stable per-item bulk-command-rejected reason strings before mutation. Also validate enfeoffment grantedByPolityId against M3 polity state, and ensure actor polity authority is derived from existing polity/office state rather than string equality alone. Add focused tests that fail on both reproduced cases, then rerun the M3 validation matrix.
